title: Forwarder Stops — Audit Pipeline Failure Response

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 2.10.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 2.10 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.24: Forwarder Stops — Audit Pipeline Failure Response.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.24' running on _internal metrics.log, splunkd health, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=_internal source=*metrics.log group=tcpout_connections earliest=-4h
| stats latest(kb) as kb by host
| join type=left host [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h "Closing tcp" OR "ERROR TcpOutputFd" | stats count as errs by host ]
| where isnull(kb) OR errs>10
| table host, kb, errs
description: Detects universal forwarder or OT collector outages that stop security-relevant events reaching the SIEM.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes _internal and sourcetypes splunkd. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Enable MC alerts; (2) map hosts to OT sites; (3) redundant intermediate HF; (4) local buffering on UF until restored. Indexes required: `index=_internal`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=splunkd`.
visualization: Table (affected sites), Single value (hosts down), Time chart.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Monitoring Console / `_internal`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` metrics.log, `splunkd` health.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enable MC alerts; (2) map hosts to OT sites; (3) redundant intermediate HF; (4) local buffering on UF until restored.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal source=*metrics.log group=tcpout_connections earliest=-4h
| stats latest(kb) as kb by host
| join type=left host [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h "Closing tcp" OR "ERROR TcpOutputFd" | stats count as errs by host ]
| where isnull(kb) OR errs>10
| table host, kb, errs
```

Understanding this SPL

**Forwarder Stops — Audit Pipeline Failure Response (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.10)** — Detects universal forwarder or OT collector outages that stop security-relevant events reaching the SIEM.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` metrics.log, `splunkd` health. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Monitoring Console / `_internal`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_internal, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **host**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(kb) OR errs>10` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: ], errs, host, kb.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (affected sites), Single value (hosts down), Time chart.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for detects universal forwarder or ot collector outages that stop security-relevant events reaching the siem. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
